mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates expanding real output, lifting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO expansions are not positive in > 50% of months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0% represents restrictive monetary policy that tightens financial conditions and raises equity discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC magnitude falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in payroll employment growth and rising unemployment rate signal a contracting labor market that reduces consumer income expectations and corporate revenue growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative returns with Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling period, or hit rate < 0.50.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions where unemployment is below 4 percent and industrial output is elevated, consecutive month-over-month declines in industrial production signal an impending growth inflection that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion characterized by low unemployment and elevated industrial output
failure_condition: During periods with UNRATE below 4.0% and INDPRO contractions, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations, or Rank IC is not negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales growth signals robust consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and positive earnings surprises, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS momentum fails to coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in > 50% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising consumer prices and an elevated federal funds rate indicate a sharply positive real policy rate that tightens liquidity and compresses corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising CPIAUCSL and elevated FEDFUNDS does not produce negative 60-day returns with Rank IC < -0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over two months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household spending power and trigger risk-off positioning in equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE increases > 0.3pp in 2 months, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter contraction in real GDP indicates a broad demand slowdown that lowers earnings expectations and triggers de-risking across equity markets, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Following a quarterly GDPC1 decline, S&P 500 60-day returns are not negative in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC magnitude is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
